title: Organize and push current local changes
description: Commit the existing working-tree changes in coherent groups, run available quality gates, and push main. This issue tracks the publishing/session-handoff work rather than owning the underlying feature design.
acceptance_criteria: Relevant changed files are grouped into intentional commits, quality gates are reported, and git status is up to date with origin/main except for no remaining local changes.
close_reason: Committed local changes in grouped commits, ran lint/json/build gates, and pushed main.

title: Update roadmap to point active task tracking at Beads
description: After migrating roadmap TODOs into Beads, docs/roadmap.md should remain strategic context rather than the durable task source. Add a concise note that active work should start from bd ready / bd show.
acceptance_criteria: Roadmap readers can tell that Beads is the source of truth for active tasks and that roadmap task lists are context, not the tracker.
close_reason: Roadmap now points active task tracking at Beads.

title: 破壊の目系または狂気系のカードを 1 枚だけ追加する
description: docs/roadmap.md と docs/status/card-pool-package-map.md に基づく、非アセット系の次タスク。新しいサブシステムは増やさず、破壊の目系または狂気系のどちらか片側だけを伸ばす新規カードを 1 枚選んで実装する。
acceptance_criteria: 新規カードが 1 枚だけ実装され、ローカライズが追加されていること。分類・状況ドキュメントはそのカードに関係する範囲だけ更新されていること。ローカライズまたは packed file に触れるため、build / install 検証の要否が明記されていること。

title: ゲームプレイ: Flandre カードプールの小さな追加単位
description: 非アセット系のゲームプレイ作業を扱う epic。各実装単位は build / install / 検証まで見通せる小ささに保つ。参照元は docs/roadmap.md の次の直近着手、docs/status/card-pool-package-map.md、docs/design/bloodshed-design-note.md。
acceptance_criteria: 子 issue は一度に 1 つの狭い挙動だけを扱うこと。実装タスクにはローカライズ、分類ドキュメント、状況ドキュメントの追従と、アセットまたは PCK 内容が変わる場合の install 検証観点を含めること。

title: Remove agent roleplay wording
description: Clean up unnecessary roleplay-style agent/member wording from project agent documentation while preserving practical workflow guidance.
close_reason: Removed roleplay-style agent role wording from project agent docs.

title: Verify Spine combat visuals after install
description: Runtime verification follow-up for the Spine scene patch. Build managed code if needed, rebuild PCK, install the mod, restart the game, and verify Flandre combat display through the documented install gate and live visual checks.
acceptance_criteria: Report exact build/install/MCP or live-check steps used, including success/failure and returned values or exact errors where MCP is involved.

title: Implement the chosen Bloodshed extension slice
description: Implementation follow-up for the Bloodshed design decision. Keep the slice to one new behavior, such as a rare spender or a single per-N payoff, and avoid combining consumption with multiple threshold tiers.
acceptance_criteria: The implementation matches the resolved design issue, includes localization/docs updates, and plans build/install verification for changed packed content.

title: Apply Flandre SpineSprite scene patch
description: Run tools/Enable-FlandreSpineVisuals.ps1 once the required Spine exports exist, producing flandre_skel_data.tres and replacing the combat scene Visuals node from Sprite2D to SpineSprite.
acceptance_criteria: Scene changes are generated by the documented script, backup/output paths are checked, and the diff is limited to the Spine visual migration.

title: Prepare required Flandre Spine export assets
description: Spine migration prerequisite from docs/visuals/flandre-spine-visual-entry.md. Prepare a local export folder containing exactly flandre.atlas, flandre.skel, and flandre.png, or document the blocker if the art pipeline/Meshy retrieval is not ready.
acceptance_criteria: All three required files are available with expected names, or the issue clearly records what external asset step is blocking them.

title: Fix combat animation workflow hot-reload wording
description: docs/status/combat-animation-progress.md still says to build and hot-reload after enabling Spine, but AGENTS.md and docs/agent-knowledge.md say hot_reload_project is not valid in this workspace. Update the workflow text to build, install, and restart instead.
acceptance_criteria: Combat animation docs no longer tell agents to use hot_reload_project or ambiguous hot-reload wording for this workspace.

title: Reconcile the next Bloodshed slice direction
description:
## Decision
Pending. Decide whether the next Bloodshed implementation should be a rare spender, another per-N scaling payoff, or a different single-behavior slice.

## Rationale
The current docs mention post-Bloodshed directions at different levels of specificity after BloodPactCard, ScarletAppetiteCard, and SanguineGuardCard. A decision issue keeps the implementation issue from mixing consumption, scaling, and threshold tiers in one slice.

## Alternatives Considered
- Rare spender that consumes current Bloodshed.
- Non-consuming per-N scaling payoff that uses attack or draw instead of block.
- Stricter self-HP-loss payoff if the STS2 API exposes a reliable card-paid HP hook.
acceptance_criteria: Decision records the chosen next behavior, the reason, and which implementation issue should carry it.

title: Review Madness target-change patch side effects
description: Roadmap mid-term risk: Madness and target-changing cards currently rely on common Harmony behavior. Review the current shared patch boundary and identify whether it remains acceptable or needs narrowing before more target-changing cards are added.
acceptance_criteria: Review separates confirmed behavior from inferred risk and produces either a no-change note or a scoped follow-up implementation issue.

title: Repair remaining mojibake in project docs
description: docs/roadmap.md lists remaining mojibake as a risk, and docs/agent-knowledge.md still contains unreadable role/oversight text. Repair only text whose intended meaning can be recovered from nearby docs or current workflow rules; otherwise preserve uncertainty explicitly.
acceptance_criteria: Unreadable sections that affect handoff or operating rules are made readable, with unrecoverable content marked as unknown rather than guessed.

title: Visuals: migrate Flandre combat presentation to Spine
description: Roadmap migration epic for replacing the current Sprite2D combat presentation with Spine-based visuals. Source context: docs/roadmap.md, docs/status/combat-animation-progress.md, docs/visuals/flandre-spine-visual-entry.md.
acceptance_criteria: Required Spine exports are prepared, scene patching is performed through the documented script, and completion is verified after build/PCK/install/restart rather than repository edits alone.

title: Operations/docs: stabilize verification and handoff
description: Roadmap migration epic for documentation cleanup, verification repeatability, and handoff reliability. Source context: docs/roadmap.md risks and operating rules, docs/agent-knowledge.md.
acceptance_criteria: Open work that used to live as roadmap/risk TODOs is represented as Beads issues; docs changes point agents at Beads as the durable task source.

title: Stabilize Reflection/random-generation verification path
description: Roadmap mid-term task: keep gameplay verification for Reflection-style and random-generation cards reliable with localization included, so generated choices and pool names remain readable during live verification.
acceptance_criteria: A repeatable verification path exists for Reflection/random-generation flows, including localization checks for card/pool names.

title: Plan future Flandre mechanics beyond current axes
description:
## Decision
Pending. Consider up to two future mechanics beyond Destruction Eye, Madness, and Bloodshed, then decide whether any should become implementation work.

## Rationale
The roadmap asks for future expansion room, but the current card pool already has multiple active axes. This decision should prevent premature subsystem growth before existing lanes are stable.

## Alternatives Considered
- Delay new mechanics until Eye, Madness, and Bloodshed have broader verification coverage.
- Add one lightweight mechanic that reuses current damage or targeting hooks.
- Add no new mechanic and deepen existing lanes instead.
acceptance_criteria: A short design note records candidate mechanics, why they fit or do not fit, and whether any should become implementation work.
